mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 3 Series 335d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 335d engine typically costs £4,000–£7,500 supply only, with the N57 and B57 variants at the higher end. Fitting adds £900–£1,500 depending on the specialist. A fully remanufactured unit from a dedicated specialist can cost from £7,500 upwards. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your 3 Series.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 3 Series 335d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which generation you have. The M57 (E90, 2006–2011) is a cast-iron classic — robust but with swirl flap vulnerability. The N57 (F30, 2012–2019) offers more power (309 bhp) but has rear-mounted timing chain concerns. The B57 (G20, 2020–present) is the most refined and reliable. All generations require regular maintenance and DPF/EGR vigilance.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 3 Series 335d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Swirl flap failure on the M57 (E90) — plastic flaps can break and destroy the engine. Timing chain stretch on the N57 (F30) — rear-mounted chain is expensive to replace. DPF and EGR clogging across all generations. The B57 (G20) is significantly improved.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a 335d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the generation and vehicle condition. Against a well-kept F30 worth £8,000–£15,000, a £5,000–£7,000 recon is 30–70% of the car's value — borderline but often worth it. Against a tired E90 worth £4,000–£6,000, the same recon may exceed our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. G20 values (£15,000–£25,000) make replacement more viable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 3 Series 335d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: E90/E91/E92 (2006–2011): M57D30TU2. F30/F31/F34 (2012–2019): N57D30T1. G20 (2020–present): B57D30. Check the VIN plate or vehicle documentation.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N57 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The chain must be replaced — it's a rear-mounted chain that requires engine removal for access. A preventative replacement costs £1,500–£3,000. A snapped chain almost always destroys the engine, requiring a £5,000–£7,500 replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

rows.eraNote: Cast-iron block twin-turbo — swirl flap failure is the critical weakness; otherwise robust
Aluminium block; 309 bhp, 630 Nm; timing chain rear-mounted; EGR/DPF issues
Modular B57 engine; improved reliability; chain and cooling system revised

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 335d? Check These First
buyIf: * M57-engined car (E90) with swirl flaps replaced with metal items or deleted
* N57-engined car (F30) with documented timing chain history (or B57 in G20)
* Full service history with regular oil changes (critical for chain longevity)
* Evidence of regular longer runs (DPF health)
avoidIf: * M57 with no swirl flap history — assume they're original plastic and budget £300–£600
* N57 with cold-start rattle and no chain history — assume it's overdue
* No service history — both M57 and N57 are unforgiving of neglect
* Short-journey ownership history — DPF and EGR issues likely
* Unexplained coolant loss — may indicate EGR cooler or head gasket issues

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 335d is BMW's flagship 3.0-litre twin-turbo diesel — a genuine performance icon across three generations. The M57 (E90) is a cast-iron classic with swirl flap vulnerability — address that and it's a robust engine. The N57 (F30) offers more power but has rear-mounted timing chain concerns — budget for chain maintenance if the history is unclear. The B57 (G20) is the modern, improved version with fewer known issues. With typical values ranging from £6,000 (E90) to £20,000+ (G20), an engine replacement is a significant but often worthwhile investment on cherished examples.

cta.label: → Compare 335d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 3 Series 335d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling (N57 timing chain warning) — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine era (M57, N57 or B57) and oil-change discipline
* Swirl Flaps — For M57 (E90): confirm replacement with metal flaps or deletion
* Timing Chain — For N57 (F30): check for any chain work or cold-start rattle
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear on all diesel eras
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate EGR cooler or injector issues
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or grinding? (twin-turbo setup)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
